# UC-18.2.18 · NSX Intelligence Recommended Firewall Rule Publish Queue

## Description

Correlates NSX Intelligence service posture (`vmware:nsx:intelligence` mirroring `GET /api/v1/intelligence/status`) with security-recommendation backlog rows (`vmware:nsx:recommendation` fields `recommendation_id`, `status`, `rule_count`, `publish_timestamp`, `error_message`), Policy realization touches (`vmware:nsx:policy` paths referencing segmentation or recommendation URIs), audit trails (`vmware:nsx:audit` `request_uri` families under `/infra/security-recommendations`), and vCenter maintenance context (`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`) so teams detect when micro-segmentation recommendations wait unpublished—queue depth, stuck `PENDING`/`APPROVED` transitions, or `FAILED` publishes that keep east-west access wider than planners intended.

## Value

Backlogged recommendations are a schedule and risk story: each hour least-privilege rows wait on humans or broken publish paths, audit narratives stay wider, compensating controls cost more, and incident teams inherit noisy east-west graphs. Splunk collapses Intelligence health, queue depth, last operator touches (`last_rec_actor`), Policy realization timestamps, and coarse vCenter motion into one `site_key` row so CAB spends time on targeted publishes instead of guessing whether analytics, Policy, or process owns the delay.

## Implementation

Stand up HTTPS collectors or TA modular inputs for `GET /api/v1/intelligence/status` and `GET /policy/api/v1/infra/security-recommendations` into `vmware:nsx:intelligence` and `vmware:nsx:recommendation`; keep Policy, audit, and vCenter feeds aligned on `site_key`; schedule saved search `uc_18_2_18_intel_publish_queue` every 15–30 minutes with `nsx_intel_planned_publish_window.csv` throttles; publish `ds_intel_queue_site` in Dashboard Studio.

## Evidence

Saved search **`uc_18_2_18_intel_publish_queue`** over `vmware:nsx:intelligence` joined to `vmware:nsx:recommendation`, Dashboard Studio workbook **`ds_intel_queue_site`** keyed by `site_key`, weekly CSV exports of `recommendation_backlog` / `rules_in_flight`, and NSX Intelligence UI screenshots for the same window.

## Detailed Implementation

**Prerequisites**
• **Intelligence licensing** — Confirm NSX Intelligence node and flow-analytics entitlements on Broadcom SKUs before dashboards blame people for saturation that is really collector budget math.
• **API RBAC** — Create a read-only principal that can call `GET /api/v1/intelligence/status` and `GET /policy/api/v1/infra/security-recommendations` (follow child hrefs your automation needs) without DELETE/PUT privileges; rotate secrets through Splunk credential stanzas only.
• **Splunk TA footprint** — Install **`Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt`** ([Splunkbase **4856**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) anywhere `vmware:nsx:intelligence`, `vmware:nsx:recommendation`, `vmware:nsx:policy`, and `vmware:nsx:audit` parse against NSX 4.2 / 4.1 Policy APIs.
• **vCenter context** — Layer **`Splunk_TA_vmware`** ([Splunkbase **1810**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) so `vmware:vsphere:vcenter` captures maintenance, vMotion, and evacuations that relocate Intelligence or Manager VMs.
• **Syslog scale** — Land noisy Intelligence appliance `/var/log` streams on **Splunk Connect for Syslog** ([Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) with parsers that honor the same TZ as NSX NTP sources.
• **ACI hybrid (optional)** — Add **`Splunk_TA_cisco-aci`** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) and executive panels from **Cisco Data Center Networking** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) only when east-west queue stories must sit beside `cisco:aci:syslog` EPG evidence.
• **Prerequisite pairing** — Keep **`UC-18.2.7`** in service so intent-vs-realized `revision_id` baselines exist before every `FAILED` publish is treated as an Intelligence defect.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
(1) **`vmware:nsx:intelligence`** — Modular input or scripted poller hits `GET /api/v1/intelligence/status` every 60–300 seconds with jitter, emitting JSON lines carrying `service_status`, `cluster_id`, `error_message`, `component_status`, and any flow-collector health children your build exposes.
(2) **`vmware:nsx:recommendation`** — Page `GET /policy/api/v1/infra/security-recommendations` (add domain-scoped paths if Policy is sharded) into events with normalized `recommendation_id`, `status`, `rule_count`, `publish_timestamp`, `error_message`, `display_name`, and `sequence_number` when the API returns them.
(3) **`vmware:nsx:policy`** — Continue segmentation realization exports so `resource_path` retains security-recommendation hrefs while publish storms run; those URIs feed the merged policy join in the SPL.
(4) **`vmware:nsx:audit`** — Forward unsampled `request_uri`, `operation`, `user_name`, `client_ip`, and `outcome` whenever operators approve, reject, or republish recommendations from UI or automation clients.
(5) **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`** — Include tasks/events for Intelligence appliance MoIDs so maintenance windows explain benign backlog spikes; map MoID\u2194`site_key` via `intelligence_appliance_site_map.csv` when you outgrow the coarse `join_one` hint.
(6) **Site key hygiene** — Maintain `nsx_site_normalization.csv` so `manager_cluster` + `domain_name` tuples hash to the same `site_key` across sourcetypes even when one feed emits `default` and another emits `mgw`.

**Step 2 — Create the search**
Save macro **`uc_18_2_18_intel_publish_queue`** with index override arguments, then paste the SPL block below into Search before promoting it to the scheduled alert.

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.18 intel publish queue")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:intelligence" earliest=-6h latest=now
| eval site_key=trim(lower(coalesce(nsx_site_id,site_id,domain_name,manager_cluster,"default")))
| eval intel_state=upper(trim(coalesce(service_status,cluster_status,status,"")))
| stats latest(intel_state) as intelligence_aggregate_state by site_key
| join type=left site_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:recommendation" earliest=-24h latest=now
  | eval site_key=trim(lower(coalesce(nsx_site_id,site_id,domain_name,manager_cluster,"default")))
  | eval rec_id=coalesce(recommendation_id,recommendation_uuid,id)
  | eval rec_status=upper(trim(coalesce(status,recommendation_status,"")))
  | eval rule_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(rule_count,pending_rules_count,"0"))
  | eval err_txt=coalesce(error_message,failure_reason,last_error,"")
  | where rec_status IN ("PENDING","QUEUED","APPROVED","WAITING","FAILED","ERROR") OR match(rec_status,"(?i)pend|queue|approv|fail")
  | stats dc(rec_id) as recommendation_backlog sum(rule_cnt) as rules_in_flight latest(eval(if(match(rec_status,"(?i)FAIL|ERROR|REJECT"),err_txt,null()))) as latest_queue_failure_excerpt max(_time) as last_recommendation_event by site_key ]
| join type=left site_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) (sourcetype="vmware:nsx:policy" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsx:audit") earliest=-24h latest=now
  | eval site_key=trim(lower(coalesce(nsx_site_id,site_id,domain_name,manager_cluster,"default")))
  | eval is_pol=if(sourcetype="vmware:nsx:policy",1,0)
  | eval uri=lower(coalesce(resource_path,policy_path,path,request_uri,""))
  | where (is_pol==1 AND match(uri,"(?i)recommend|segment")) OR (is_pol==0 AND match(uri,"(?i)security-recommend|publish"))
  | stats max(eval(if(is_pol==1,_time,null()))) as last_policy_realize_event max(eval(if(is_pol==0,_time,null()))) as last_security_rec_audit latest(eval(if(is_pol==0,user_name,null()))) as last_rec_actor by site_key ]
| eval join_one=1
| join type=left join_one [ search index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:vcenter" earliest=-8h latest=now
  | where match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maintenance|vmotion|nsx") | stats latest(_time) as vcenter_correlated_motion_ts | eval join_one=1 ]
| fields - join_one
| fillnull value=0 recommendation_backlog rules_in_flight
| fillnull value="" latest_queue_failure_excerpt intelligence_aggregate_state
| eval svc_degraded=match(lower(intelligence_aggregate_state),"(?i)down|degraded|error|fail|not_ready")
| eval fail_signal=if(svc_degraded==1 OR (len(latest_queue_failure_excerpt)>3 AND latest_queue_failure_excerpt!="") OR match(lower(latest_queue_failure_excerpt),"(?i)fail|error|denied|conflict|rollback"),1,0)
| eval backlog_hot=recommendation_backlog>=15 OR rules_in_flight>=50
| where backlog_hot=1 OR fail_signal==1 OR (recommendation_backlog>=5 AND last_recommendation_event<relative_time(now(),"-8h") AND last_recommendation_event>0)
| eval severity=if(fail_signal==1 AND backlog_hot==1,100,if(fail_signal==1,88,if(backlog_hot==1,72,65)))
| eval story=site_key." | bl=".recommendation_backlog." | r=".rules_in_flight." | ".intelligence_aggregate_state
| sort - severity - last_recommendation_event | head 200
| rename latest_queue_failure_excerpt as last_queue_failure_excerpt
| table site_key severity intelligence_aggregate_state recommendation_backlog rules_in_flight last_recommendation_event last_queue_failure_excerpt last_rec_actor story
```

**Understanding this SPL**
• **Isolation** — `site_key` keeps lab, tenant, and shared-manager domains apart so a synthetic spike never masquerades as production risk on the same panel.
• **Intelligence first** — The leading `vmware:nsx:intelligence` stats arm proves analytics services are healthy before CAB is paged for missing human approvals.
• **Backlog math** — `dc(rec_id)` approximates unique open recommendations; swap to `approx_dc` when cardinality threatens search memory limits documented for your cluster size.
• **Rule pressure** — `rules_in_flight` sums `rule_count` only for statuses that still imply unpublished work (`PENDING`, `APPROVED`, `FAILED`, etc. per the `where` clause).
• **Merged policy + audit** — One join reproduces both `last_policy_realize_event` and `last_rec_actor`, avoiding duplicate `site_key` subsearches while preserving five sourcetypes overall.
• **vCenter hint** — The `join_one` pattern timestamps any maintenance motion observed in the window so benign ESXi work is visible on day one without perfect per-host lookups.
• **Ranking** — `severity` prioritizes combined service faults plus deep queues ahead of shallow noise so bridges open with vendor hypotheses first.

**Step 3 — Validate**
(1) **UI parity** — NSX Intelligence Recommended Rules counts should match `recommendation_backlog` within one collector interval for each `site_key`.
(2) **API truth** — Use Policy `GET` on a failing `recommendation_id` and confirm `error_message` text matches `last_queue_failure_excerpt` after the rename.
(3) **Audit parity** — Filter `vmware:nsx:audit` for `/infra/security-recommendations` URIs and verify timestamps align with `last_rec_actor`.
(4) **Realization pause** — If `last_policy_realize_event` flatlines while backlog grows, inspect Manager/Corfu realization health (pair **`UC-18.2.13`**) before closing the incident as analytics-only.
(5) **Negative control** — During healthy operations, raise macro thresholds until the search returns zero rows so paging noise stays predictable.

**Step 4 — Operationalize**
(1) **Schedule** — Run every 15 minutes with throttle keys from `nsx_intel_planned_publish_window.csv` (`site_key`, `chg_id`, maint owner).
(2) **Dashboard Studio** — Publish `ds_intel_queue_site` with tokens `tok_site_key`, `tok_threshold_bl`, `tok_threshold_rules`, and `tok_time` for NOC tiles.
(3) **Ticketing** — Push `story`, `recommendation_backlog`, `rules_in_flight`, and `last_rec_actor` into ServiceNow with deep links to Intelligence UI filters.
(4) **Ownership** — Restrict macro edits to `splunk_role_nsx_seg` and document escalation contacts in `nsx_intel_escalation.csv`.
(5) **Evidence vault** — Archive weekly CSV extracts of the alerting table plus NSX UI screenshots for segmentation governance decks.

**Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot**
**Visualization layout** — Row A: single values for max `recommendation_backlog`, max `rules_in_flight`, and count of `severity=100`. Row B: multiline timechart per `site_key` for backlog and rule pressure. Row C: drilldown table (`site_key`, `severity`, `intelligence_aggregate_state`, `last_queue_failure_excerpt`, `last_rec_actor`, `story`) wired to raw `vmware:nsx:recommendation` searches.
**Alert design** — Fire when `severity>=88` and result count > 0; throttle four hours per `site_key`; include adaptive response snippets that attach the last three recommendation `error_message` samples from a correlated `join` subsearch for bridge engineers.
**Troubleshooting A** — Intelligence status shows fault but backlog flat: recycle Intelligence services per VMware documentation, validate analytics disk, rerun status poll.
**Troubleshooting B** — Deep queue, healthy status: inspect `APPROVED` rows lacking `PUBLISHED`; Policy realization or transport programming usually owns the fault—pair **`UC-18.2.13`** telemetry.
**Troubleshooting C** — `CONFLICT` or `DENIED` excerpts: diff operator edits vs. recommendation payload with **`UC-18.2.7`** revision artifacts.
**Troubleshooting D** — Audit quiet but backlog grows: look for HTTP 429 collectors, expired API tokens, or RBAC scope loss rather than malice.
**Troubleshooting E** — `vcenter_correlated_motion_ts` updates during spikes: confirm ESXi maintenance before sev-1 declarations.
**Cadence** — Quarterly reconcile Splunk backlog histograms with CAB minutes so executives trust the signal.

**Instrumentation checklist** — After each TA upgrade run `fieldsummary` on **`vmware:nsx:intelligence`**, **`vmware:nsx:recommendation`**, **`vmware:nsx:policy`**, **`vmware:nsx:audit`**, and **`vmware:vsphere:vcenter`**, confirming API fields `service_status`, `recommendation_id`, `publish_timestamp`, `resource_path`, `request_uri`, and vCenter maintenance markers still populate; smoke-test derived columns **`recommendation_backlog`**, **`rules_in_flight`**, **`fail_signal`**, **`backlog_hot`**, **`svc_degraded`**, **`severity`**, **`story`**, **`last_queue_failure_excerpt`**, **`last_rec_actor`**, **`last_policy_realize_event`**, **`last_security_rec_audit`**, **`vcenter_correlated_motion_ts`**, **`site_key`**, **`join_one`**, **`is_pol`**, **`uri`**, **`rec_id`**, **`rec_status`**, **`intel_state`**, **`rule_cnt`**, **`err_txt`**, **`latest_queue_failure_excerpt`**.

**Glossary** — **Publish queue** means ML-suggested DFW rows awaiting approvals and realization. **site_key** ties feeds per Manager cluster plus domain tuple. **join_one** is the coarse maintenance breadcrumb. **Corfu** is the metadata fabric backing Policy writes.

**Sibling handoff** — Pair **`UC-18.2.14`**, **`UC-18.2.1`**, **`UC-18.2.7`**, and **`UC-18.2.13`** before declaring a segmentation defeat that belongs to analytics, flows, drift, or Manager health respectively.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-18.2.18 intel publish queue")`
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:intelligence" earliest=-6h latest=now
| eval site_key=trim(lower(coalesce(nsx_site_id,site_id,domain_name,manager_cluster,"default")))
| eval intel_state=upper(trim(coalesce(service_status,cluster_status,status,"")))
| stats latest(intel_state) as intelligence_aggregate_state by site_key
| join type=left site_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsx:recommendation" earliest=-24h latest=now
  | eval site_key=trim(lower(coalesce(nsx_site_id,site_id,domain_name,manager_cluster,"default")))
  | eval rec_id=coalesce(recommendation_id,recommendation_uuid,id)
  | eval rec_status=upper(trim(coalesce(status,recommendation_status,"")))
  | eval rule_cnt=tonumber(coalesce(rule_count,pending_rules_count,"0"))
  | eval err_txt=coalesce(error_message,failure_reason,last_error,"")
  | where rec_status IN ("PENDING","QUEUED","APPROVED","WAITING","FAILED","ERROR") OR match(rec_status,"(?i)pend|queue|approv|fail")
  | stats dc(rec_id) as recommendation_backlog sum(rule_cnt) as rules_in_flight latest(eval(if(match(rec_status,"(?i)FAIL|ERROR|REJECT"),err_txt,null()))) as latest_queue_failure_excerpt max(_time) as last_recommendation_event by site_key ]
| join type=left site_key [ search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) (sourcetype="vmware:nsx:policy" OR sourcetype="vmware:nsx:audit") earliest=-24h latest=now
  | eval site_key=trim(lower(coalesce(nsx_site_id,site_id,domain_name,manager_cluster,"default")))
  | eval is_pol=if(sourcetype="vmware:nsx:policy",1,0)
  | eval uri=lower(coalesce(resource_path,policy_path,path,request_uri,""))
  | where (is_pol==1 AND match(uri,"(?i)recommend|segment")) OR (is_pol==0 AND match(uri,"(?i)security-recommend|publish"))
  | stats max(eval(if(is_pol==1,_time,null()))) as last_policy_realize_event max(eval(if(is_pol==0,_time,null()))) as last_security_rec_audit latest(eval(if(is_pol==0,user_name,null()))) as last_rec_actor by site_key ]
| eval join_one=1
| join type=left join_one [ search index=vmware sourcetype="vmware:vsphere:vcenter" earliest=-8h latest=now
  | where match(lower(_raw),"(?i)maintenance|vmotion|nsx") | stats latest(_time) as vcenter_correlated_motion_ts | eval join_one=1 ]
| fields - join_one
| fillnull value=0 recommendation_backlog rules_in_flight
| fillnull value="" latest_queue_failure_excerpt intelligence_aggregate_state
| eval svc_degraded=match(lower(intelligence_aggregate_state),"(?i)down|degraded|error|fail|not_ready")
| eval fail_signal=if(svc_degraded==1 OR (len(latest_queue_failure_excerpt)>3 AND latest_queue_failure_excerpt!="") OR match(lower(latest_queue_failure_excerpt),"(?i)fail|error|denied|conflict|rollback"),1,0)
| eval backlog_hot=recommendation_backlog>=15 OR rules_in_flight>=50
| where backlog_hot=1 OR fail_signal==1 OR (recommendation_backlog>=5 AND last_recommendation_event<relative_time(now(),"-8h") AND last_recommendation_event>0)
| eval severity=if(fail_signal==1 AND backlog_hot==1,100,if(fail_signal==1,88,if(backlog_hot==1,72,65)))
| eval story=site_key." | bl=".recommendation_backlog." | r=".rules_in_flight." | ".intelligence_aggregate_state
| sort - severity - last_recommendation_event | head 200
| rename latest_queue_failure_excerpt as last_queue_failure_excerpt
| table site_key severity intelligence_aggregate_state recommendation_backlog rules_in_flight last_recommendation_event last_queue_failure_excerpt last_rec_actor story
```

## Visualization

(1) Single value — max `recommendation_backlog` per `site_key`. (2) Column chart — `rules_in_flight` stacked by `intelligence_aggregate_state`. (3) Table — `site_key`, `severity`, `last_queue_failure_excerpt`, `last_rec_actor`. (4) Timechart — `recommendation_backlog` vs `last_security_rec_audit` (from joined fields). (5) Alert drilldown — deep link to NSX Intelligence UI filtered on `recommendation_id`.

## Known False Positives

**bulk_approval_batching** — CAB windows that approve dozens of recommendations in one REST burst temporarily inflate `recommendation_backlog` while `PUBLISHED` events trail by minutes; require two polls trending the same direction before paging and anchor suppressions to change records citing Intelligence freeze exceptions.

**planned_intel_cluster_upgrade** — Rolling Intelligence patches set `intelligence_aggregate_state` to transient DEGRADED strings even though ingest paused by design; tie `vcenter_correlated_motion_ts` and known maint MOIDs to mute macros keyed by `site_key`.

**policy_coordinator_backpressure** — Large `rule_count` bundles wait on Policy/Corfu realization during bulk segmentation imports, so `rules_in_flight` spikes without Intelligence faults; compare `last_policy_realize_event` stalls against Manager cluster health (pair UC-18.2.13) before blaming analytics alone.

**audit_uri_sampling_gaps** — Sampled audit streams drop `/security-recommendations` URIs so `last_rec_actor` looks stale while UI work continues; validate full-fidelity audit archives before accusing teams of inaction.

**false_failed_status_strings** — Vendors sometimes emit `error_message=OK_WITH_WARNINGS` or localized tokens containing literal "fail" substrings; normalize with `translate` lookups and require backlog depth plus vendor faults together.

**lab_domain_low_priority** — Engineering tenants keep `PENDING` queues open for weeks while production drains cleanly; tag `site_key` with environment metadata or `nsx_intel_queue_suppress.csv` so prod owns the pager.

**duplicate_rec_id_hashes** — Collector bugs re-ingest the same `recommendation_id` under different `_cd`, inflating `dc(rec_id)`; dedupe at index time with stable `orig_key` hashes.
